title: Backend Load Remediation - Cosmos Partition Alignment for Feedback and Reports
scope: feedback container access patterns, report aggregation, Cosmos RU efficiency
problem: Current Cosmos queries read feedback and report data using predicates that do not align well with the configured partition key, which can cause unnecessary cross-partition scans and high RU usage.
risk: User report generation and feedback listing can become disproportionately expensive as data grows, especially for multi-session users and admin-style list views.

evidence.note: Feedback container is created with partition key /agent_type.
Feedback list query orders by _ts across the container using TOP {limit}, which is expensive on a non-time partition.
Per-session feedback aggregation queries by session_id even though the container partitions by agent_type.
/api/report/me fans out feedback and checklist aggregation across all session_ids for a user.

goals: Align read paths with partition strategy or adjust storage/query design.
Prevent report generation from scaling linearly into many expensive feedback scans.
Keep RU usage predictable as feedback volume grows.

implementation_plan.action: Choose a partition-safe strategy for feedback reads.
details: Preferred option: repartition feedback by session_id if session-based lookups are the dominant access pattern.
Alternative option: maintain a pre-aggregated per-session report document so report generation does not need to scan feedback items live.
If migration is deferred, at minimum constrain list endpoints and document the cross-partition cost.
Avoid one live feedback scan per session when generating a user-wide report.
Consider aggregating once per user request from a cheaper event source or reading from cached/materialized summaries.
Add a hard upper bound to limit and keep administrative list size small.
If ordering by _ts remains necessary, scope it to a partition or a small maintained summary store rather than the whole container.

implementation_plan.action: Document and validate any migration path.
details: If repartitioning is chosen, include a data backfill or dual-read strategy.
Keep local fallback behavior working unless explicitly out of scope.

acceptance_criteria: Feedback and report queries no longer rely on expensive cross-partition scans for common paths, or the hot paths are moved to pre-aggregated storage.
/api/report/me no longer performs unbounded per-session expensive feedback fan-out.
Administrative feedback listing has a clear and enforced ceiling.
Tests or smoke validation confirm report output remains correct.
verification: Add tests for any new aggregation helper or summary storage path.
If Cosmos metrics are available, compare RU usage before and after on representative report queries.
execution_notes_for_claude_code: This item may require a pragmatic staged fix. If repartitioning is too invasive for one pass, implement a bounded stopgap plus a documented migration plan in code comments or follow-up notes.
